title: Affordability of a diet depends on cost and income
subtitle: Cost (PPP $) for energy sufficient, nutrient-adequate and healthy diets

scenes.text: In 1990, nearly [emphasis: 2 billion people] worldwide lived in extreme poverty – in other words, on less than $2.15 per day.

Over the same period, the world’s population increased by around 2.4 billion people and the number of people with incomes above the international poverty line [emphasis: increased from 3.3 billion to 7 billion]. As a result, the share of the global population living in extreme poverty was reduced from about 38 percent to 8.5 percent.
